items.title: Missing EOB
items.content:
For Availity EOBs

  1. Select correct state.
  2. Go to claim status

Once in the claim status Select the payer (Example BCBS TX)

I will then go to the date I am working on in the ON Notification queue, filter by payor and use the NPI from the queue.

This usually works. It is does not, I will also try this number as a last attempt




For the claims in DUE TODAY, if we are missing EOBs and there is a case note by Document team that missing pathlink and have no way to locate EOB, you may proceed on the claim AS IS. We don't need to wait until EOD for the document.


Christus HIX Plan - use the date on the file information.  Reference: Link

items.title: Timing
items.content:
Prioritize reviewing the EOB to check the EOB date if the claim is truly TIMING. If the Production Date is Timing and the Check Date is not. Use the Check date to send the Negotiations. If both date is Timing, don't change the EOB date and make sure to add case notes to verify the Timing date before closing the claim




Reference: Link

items.title: PIF Process
items.content:
Paid in Full - If the Charge amount matches the Paid Amount + Patient Responsibility

High Reimbursement - If the Initial Allowed Amount is Higher than the NSA Request Offer

Below Threshold - If the Charge Amount is below $300

Below Threshold - If the Difference between the NSA Request Offer and Initial Allowed amount is below $300

Reference Link

items.title: Cigna Boilermaker
items.content:
Cigna group number 3334628. It is extremely important that these are escalated immediately for payor update. As some boilermakers get ingested as Cigna Health and Life Insurance Company. Again, this group number 3334628 is for Cigna Boilermakers .

Reference: Link 

Reference: Link

items.title: ONN Remark Codes
items.content:
N864 - This claim is subject to the No Surprises Act provisions that apply to emergency services. 
Reference: Link

N859 - The Federal No Surprise Billing Act was applied to the processing of this claim. Payment amounts are eligible for dispute pursuant to any Federal documented appeal/ grievance/ dispute resolution process(es).

N860 - The Federal No Surprise Billing Act Qualified Payment Amount (QPA) was used to calculate the member cost share(s).

N877 - This initial payment is provided in accordance with the No Surprises Act. The provider or facility may initiate open negotiation if they desire to negotiate a higher out-of-network rate.

items.title: Postal FEP Pacific
items.content:
For FEP Pacific customers: update to Blue Shield FEP if the claim has an initial 25 claim number, an R policy, and California state. 
Reference: Link

items.title: TRS / HEALTHSELECT
items.content:
TRS / HealthSelect showing as OOS may still be processed. TRS and HealthSelect are fully insured only in the state of Texas. If a claim has a TRS or HealthSelect policy number and the state is Oklahoma, it is considered OOS for NSA. 
Reference: Link

items.title: Ambetter and BAYLOR & SCOTT claims
items.content:
EOB remark codes surpass the funding type. It is very important to go by how the EOB was processed. Exception: Ambetter and Baylor & Scott. 
Reference: Link

items.title: FEP or FEDERAL FEP
items.content:
Always make queries with the POC even if instructions exist from the payor mapping team on how to identify an FEP claim. 
Reference: Link

deletedItems.title: BCBS KANSAS
deletedItems.content:
Always review the EOB

 

BCBS Kansas
- Review the EOB to ensure the payor is BLUE CROSS & BLUE SHIELD
- Review the Ins Card/ FEP Insurance
- Highmark and CareFirst will not go to BCBS Kansas
- Review HCFA 
Form 1500 will go to BCBS Kansas Professional
Form UB-04 will go to BCBS Kansas Institutional

 

BCBS Kansas City (MO) & Missouri
Payer ID 47171
- Prefixes: YBC, BN, YBP, GPS

 

BCBS Missouri
Check state if Missouri
Review the EOB and Ins Card
Check the HCFA for the form number

BCBS Kansas/Missouri Routing Decision Tree
Kansas & Missouri.docx

deletedItems.title: Good Evening Everyone! Happy FRIDAYYY🤩! Please carefully read and acknowledge all updates below!
deletedItems.content:
Today's EOB date is 02/13/2026

  • For payor related questions please tag Brooke Clayton
  • For breakdowns please tag Christy Hildreth
  • For Inpatient tag Sherrilyn Andy

FOR BCBS OOS POLICIES IDENTIFIED BY THE PREFIX TOOL:

  • Review all documents in the claim!
  • Insurance card, VOB, EOB.
  • Escalate any discrepancies.

Payers that need to be escalated immediately:

All Health First/Health First Health Plans

BCBS Kansas-Do not update this payor without escalating.

All Kaiser

All Allied

Ameriben 

Regence BlueCross BlueShield claims for the client Utah Valley Emergency Physicians 

 

BE ADVISED!

Effective Immediately: Every BCBS Kansas, Kansas Building Trades and BCBS Kansas City claim need to be approved by POC. No exceptions!

 

 

EFFECTIVE IMMEDIATELY: Escalate any split claims! We need to review EOBs that have two different claim numbers and two separate EOBs but Arbit has all the CPT codes. DO NOT combine claim numbers in Arbit, as we have received new directive.

Nutex Breakdowns :

  • Can not be submitted unless the money has been moved and approved by an ONN POC 
  • The amounts entered in Arbit must match the EOB exactly
  • Total billed, paid amount, patient responsibility, coinsurance, deductible, and copay must be verified and be included in Arbit (PR-1, PR-2, PR-3)
  • All patient information must be verified
  • EOBs should be uploaded to Arbit before adding the ID to the pending list
  •  Inpatient claims may be escalated directly in the chat. They do not need to be added to the breakdown pending list
  • Please only add true breakdowns.

Team please ensure no REV codes are being sent! Any codes that start with a zero should be reviewed. Look at the EOB and HCFA carefully and input all CPT codes and claim or policy numbers into Arbit correctly! 

  • Continue to escalate timing claims; we have identified many that are still eligible!
  • All high reimbursement, paid in full and below the threshold claims should be escalated
  • Prioritize and send out all claims that have EOB/VOB or NSA remarks, including N877. If you are doing VOB or adding documents and see Self Funded remarks, let's move those claims out of the queue. Keep in mind, if the claim has VOB but no EOB, send if the claim information looks good (NO PIF, NO DUPLICATE CPT CODES, NO REV CODES).
  • If you are working on VOB task, please make sure the plan type isn't blank and verified is selected to complete the VOB process.

Lastly, queue from the Notification Manager not inside the claim. This will allow you to confirm if there is a valid email BEFORE you send your claims!
